InstitutionAddress: 6363 Alvarado Court, Suite 250, San Diego, CA 92120
TaskDescription: A variant of the Eriksen flanker task (Eriksen and Eriksen, 1974) used to elicit both the ERN (error-related negativity) and LRP (lateralized readiness potential). A central arrowhead pointing to the left or right was flanked on both sides by arrowheads that pointed in the same direction (compatible trials) or the opposite direction (incompatible trials). Participants indicated the direction of the central arrowhead on each trial with a left- or right-hand buttonpress.
Instructions: In this task, a set of five arrowheads will be presented. Your task is to respond whether the arrowhead in the center of the screen is pointing to the left or right. If the central arrowhead is pointing to the left, press [LEFT_BUTTON]. If the central arrowhead is pointing to the right, press [RIGHT_BUTTON]. Please respond as quickly and accurately as possible.
